mnemonics: Think of the 'Key To Success' as building a successful DATA house: Design correctly, Assemble quality sources, Transform with care, Access easily.
keyPoints: A well-designed data model is the foundation for a successful data warehouse.
Data quality and integrity from source systems are critical for trustworthy analytics.
The ETL (Extract, Transform, Load) process must be robust and efficient to populate the warehouse reliably.
The warehouse must be designed for performance and ease of access for end-user querying and reporting tools.
commonMistakes: Underestimating the importance of data quality and cleansing, leading to 'garbage in, garbage out'.
Designing the warehouse without end-user needs in mind, creating a system that is not used.
Failing to establish and enforce a common dimensional model, leading to inconsistent data and analysis.
